name: ExtensionMeasure_ScoringUnit
title: R5: What units? (new)
purpose:
This extension is part of the cross-version definitions generated to enable use of the
element `Measure.scoringUnit` as defined in FHIR R5
in FHIR R4.

The source element is defined as:
`Measure.scoringUnit` 0..1 `CodeableConcept`

Across FHIR versions, the element set has been mapped as:
*  R5: `Measure.scoringUnit` 0..1 `CodeableConcept`

Following are the generation technical comments:
Element `Measure.scoringUnit` has a context of Measure based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `Measure`.
Element `Measure.scoringUnit` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.comment:
Element `Measure.scoringUnit` has a context of Measure based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `Measure`.
Element `Measure.scoringUnit` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
When this element is specified, implementations are expected to report measure scores in the specified units. Note that this may involve unit conversion if the expected units are different than the units of the resulting score calculation. In this case, unit conversions SHALL be performed according to the unit conversion semantics specified by UCUM.

element.definition: Defines the expected units of measure for the measure score. This element SHOULD be specified as a UCUM unit.
element.comment: When this element is specified, implementations are expected to report measure scores in the specified units. Note that this may involve unit conversion if the expected units are different than the units of the resulting score calculation. In this case, unit conversions SHALL be performed according to the unit conversion semantics specified by UCUM.
